Priority MITM target (predict_hard_bits lower bound = 15 bits). 5-min head-to-head vs high-prediction (m189b13c7, lb=29). Peak memory 135 GB vs 467 GB on the high-prediction candidate (3.5x). Predictor signal observed.
High-prediction reference for predictor-vs-solver signal. predict_hard_bits lb=29. Peak memory 467 GB (3.5x larger search space than priority candidate at lb=15). Same wall time (5min, both TIMEOUT).
Mid-curve point: predict_hard_bits lb=22, sr=60 force. Peak mem 337 GB at 5min — surprisingly higher than lb=26 (193 GB), refuting monotonic predictor-memory correlation.
Cert candidate, lb=26 sr=60 force, peak mem 193 GB at 5min. With 4-point curve: lb=15→135GB, lb=22→337GB, lb=26→193GB, lb=29→467GB. NOT monotonic.
90-min Mode B sr=60 MSB cert candidate seed=5 — UNKNOWN at 5400s. Standard encoding takes 12h on this same instance/seed; Mode B did NOT find SAT in 1/8 of that. Mode B speedup if any is <8x.
90-min Mode A sr=60 MSB cert candidate seed=5 — UNKNOWN at 5400s. Same as Mode B at this budget.
